body_txt: Segments of piping that are usually used to channel liquids or gases also make for effective clubs . Use of them as a weapon is especially common in derelict or war-torn zones where the piping is not in use and in poor repair, allowing easy acquisition. It is generally used amongst thugs and other low-lifes due to its decent sturdiness, being able to easily hurt somebody, and also very easy to acquire (usually ripped off the wall or machine, or finding one just laying on the floor in some dark alley). Made out of either lead, iron or steel as long as it's sturdy enough to bust heads with it's definitely going to be used. Sizes and lengths can vary too which helps make pipes more of a practical choice than it sounds. When not in the hands of a criminal or simpleton , this is generally a weapon of last resort. Even if the piping is still installed or way too large for someone to reasonably carry, pipes can still be used as weapons. Broken pipe ends can be used to impale a careless enemy, valves can release scalding liquids or gases, and they can make for a good surface to crack heads against. While most piping is generally featureless, weaponized pipes tend to feature elbow joints, valves and other types of plumbing joints, fittings, or adapters in order to emphasize that they are, in fact, just ripped off from a plumbing system. It may also be used to increase a pipe's effectiveness as a weapon, such as using an elbow joint as a hook and such. It all really depends on the user's creativity. Sub-Trope of Improvised Weapon , and related to Carry a Big Stick , 2xFore * For another piece of construction material used as an Improvised Weapon , Martial Arts Staff * A long length of pipe or a few connected ones can work well as an improvised staff . and Wrench Whack . * If you're looking for Pipe Wrenches.
